title: Athyrocarpus
taxonomy: Athyrocarpus belongs to the family of plants known as Anacardiaceae and is classified under the order Sapindales. It is a genus of plants that are native to Madagascar. Further classification places it under the kingdom Plantae, followed by the division Magnoliophyta, and then the class Magnoliopsida. The order Sapindales is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Additional classification under the family Anacardiaceae groups it with other trees such as mango and pistachio.
uses: Athyrocarpus is known to have medicinal properties, particularly in traditional African medicine. The plant is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its extracts are also used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Additionally, Athyrocarpus is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. However, more research is needed to confirm the full extent of its medicinal uses.
